title: Bletia flava
intro: Bletia flava is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia, known for its unique, bright yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Bletia flava include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Bletia flava is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat respiratory problems, and is also used in traditional remedies for its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties.
taxonomy: Bletia flava is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Bletia flava is a type of terrestrial orchid, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Bletia flava places it in the genus Bletia and the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification is consistent across various botanical sources.
